failedPrompts.claudeResponse: CeraVe Moisturizing Cream or Cetaphil Rich Hydrating Night Cream are fragrance-free and specifically formulated for sensitive skin. Both contain ceramides and hyaluronic acid without irritating ingredients.

failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Start with the essentials: a gentle cleanser like CeraVe, a moisturiser like Cetaphil, and SPF50. Skip everything else for 2 weeks, then add only one product at a time to identify what your skin actually needs.

failedPrompts.query: do sheet masks actually work for glowing skin
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Yes, quality sheet masks like COSRX or Leaders deliver immediate hydration and visible glow for 12-24 hours. They work best as occasional treatments before events rather than daily skincare replacements.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Yes, sheet masks can provide a temporary glow by infusing the skin with hydration and nutrients, though the effects are often short-lived.

failedPrompts.query: does face oil actually work or just skincare hype
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Face oils genuinely work for hydration and barrier repair when the right type matches your skin—squalane for dry skin, jojoba for combination. They're not hype if you choose correctly; most people benefit from a quality lightweight oil.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Face oils can be effective when chosen appropriately for your skin type, providing additional hydration and essential fatty acids often missing in regular moisturizers.

failedPrompts.query: are TikTok skincare recommendations actually good
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Most TikTok recommendations lack dermatological backing, but occasionally solid advice appears—focus on products with proven ingredients like niacinamide, ceramides, and hyaluronic acid. Verify with dermatologists or ingredient databases before buying.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: TikTok skincare recommendations can be hit or miss, so always cross-reference with reputable sources and consider personal skin needs before trying new products.

failedPrompts.query: does minimal skincare routine actually give results
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Yes, a minimal routine of cleanser, moisturizer, and SPF produces excellent results for most people within 4-6 weeks. More products don't equal better skin; consistency with essentials matters far more.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: A minimal skincare routine can be effective if it includes essential steps like cleansing, moisturizing, and sun protection adjusted to your skin's specific needs.

failedPrompts.query: what ingredients should I look for in a good face oil
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Prioritize squalane (absorbs quickly), jojoba oil (balances skin), vitamin E (antioxidant protection), and rosehip (barrier repair). Avoid heavy oils like coconut unless you have very dry, non-acne-prone skin.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: For a good face oil, look for ingredients like jojoba oil, squalane, rosehip oil, and marula oil, which provide hydration, antioxidants, and essential fatty acids.

failedPrompts.query: what causes damaged skin barrier and how do you repair it
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Over-exfoliation, harsh actives, and hot water damage the barrier; repair with CeraVe Moisturizer, squalane oil, and SPF while avoiding actives for 2-4 weeks. Rebuilding takes consistency and patience with gentle, hydrating products only.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: A damaged skin barrier can be caused by over-exfoliation, harsh products, and environmental stressors. Repair it with gentle cleansers, ceramides, and moisturizers like the La Roche-Posay Cicaplast Baume B5.
